description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: prepare and build. In the prepare stage add a prepare:image job that uses the gcr.io/kaniko-project/executor:debug image with an empty entrypoint, runs on any change to Dockerfile (always) and can also be started manually as an allowed‑failure job; it runs mkdir -p /kaniko/.docker, export CONTEXT_PATH=${CI_PROJECT_DIR}, export DOCKERFILE_PATH=${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/Dockerfile, export DESTINATION_PATH=${CI_REGISTRY}/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}, writes a Docker auth JSON to /kaniko/.docker/config.json, and executes /kaniko/executor --context ${CONTEXT_PATH} --dockerfile ${DOCKERFILE_PATH} --destination ${DESTINATION_PATH}. In the build stage add a build:documents job that uses the image ${CI_REGISTRY}/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}, runs cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILE=1 -G "Unix Makefiles", then cmake --build . --config Release, then ./build.py, then generates a .gitignore by finding files matching a Russian PDF naming pattern with find ./ -type f -printf "%P\n" | egrep "[\u0410-\u042F]{4}\.[0-9]{6}\.[0-9]{3}\ ([0-9\u0410-\u042F]+\ )?\([\u0410-\u042F\u0430-\u044F ]+\)\.pdf" > .gitignore, adds and removes the .gitignore, cleans untracked files, and publishes all untracked files as artifacts. Both jobs are tagged with docker and linux.
